message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent of all others. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correctly addresses the issue, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it, with a discriminating regression test where a code change is involved).

Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix F-004 (fix) in .github/workflows/ci.yml + requirements-core.lock
What was done: core + integration test lanes install --require-hashes from lockfiles (new requirements-core.lock, dev only) then --no-deps -e .
Regression test(s): test_ci_test_lanes_install_hash_pinned_not_live_pypi, test_core_lockfile_is_pinned_and_hashed_and_extra_free

Steps:
1. Read the actual changed file(s) at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
2. Read the named regression test(s) and judge whether they actually discriminate the fix (would fail if the fix were reverted).
3. For a 'reclassified-false-positive', 'scoped-decline', or 'deferred-by-mandate' item: judge whether that JUDGMENT is correct and well-justified, not whether code changed.
4. Decide: appropriate? complete? Overall verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested).

Be skeptical and specific. Return your verdict.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify fix F-004. Let me start by examining the changed files.

ba82429 fix(hardening-p3): batch 6 — two-sided docs-drift guards (F-038)
82f58ca fix(hardening-p3): batch 5 — performance, with two scoped declines (5 findings)
9b613d6 fix(hardening-p3): batch 4 — concurrency, resource & reasoning reliability (10 findings)
8608da4 fix(hardening-p3): batch 3 — CI / supply-chain hardening (8 findings)
2b07dfd fix(hardening-p3): batch 2 — security + observability seams (8 fixes, 1 FP)
af6ce68 fix(hardening-p3): batch 1 — methodology + pipeline integrity (12 findings)
f7fb90d docs(hardening): pass-2 merged (#157); open pass-3 with retrospective dimensions
1e3dce5 harden/pass-2: 33 production-readiness fixes (#124-#156) (#157)
1389242 harden/pass-1: 22 production-readiness fixes (assess→2-agent verify→fix→2-agent confirm) (#123)
16d8be3 fix(backends): conform qdrant/numpy/claude-sdk + langchain error-typing to best practices (#100)
cb218f6 fix(seams): conform langchain/langgraph/phoenix to official best practices (#99)
f9d8cb4 feat: complete the composition layer + public-readiness (audit close-out) (#98)

1	name: ci
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [main]
6	  pull_request:
7	
8	jobs:
9	  # Dependency-free core: the engine must lint, type-check, and test with NO extras installed
10	  # (optional backends are import-guarded; integration tests skip). This guards ADR-0006.
11	  core:
12	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
13	    strategy:
14	      matrix:
15	        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13", "3.14"]
16	    steps:
17	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
18	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
19	        with:
20	          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
21	      - name: Install (no extras, hash-pinned)
22	        run: |
23	          # Install the dev toolchain from the hashed, audited core lockfile so tests run against
24	          # EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned and the SBOM describes — not a live PyPI
25	          # resolution where a malicious patch release could execute under test (#F-004). The
26	          # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
27	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
28	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
29	          pip install --no-deps -e .
30	      - name: Lint (ruff)
31	        run: ruff check src tests
32	      - name: Format check (ruff)
33	        run: ruff format --check src tests
34	      - name: Type-check (mypy)
35	        run: mypy
36	      - name: Test (pytest)
37	        run: pytest -q
38	
39	  # Extras lane: install the real backends and run the integration suite so the backend code
40	  # paths actually execute, and mypy type-checks against the REAL (not ignored) library types.
41	  # Service-dependent tests (ollama/qdrant/postgres) skip when the service is absent in CI; the
42	  # extra-only tests (langgraph graph, mlflow lifecycle, OTel, numpy primitives, methodology
43	  # e2e) run for real.
44	  integration:
45	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
46	    services:
47	      postgres:
48	        image: postgres:16
49	        env:
50	          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
51	          POSTGRES_DB: assay
52	        ports: ["5432:5432"]
53	        options: >-
54	          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U postgres"
55	          --health-interval 5s --health-timeout 5s --health-retries 10
56	      qdrant:
57	        image: qdrant/qdrant:v1.12.4
58	        ports: ["6333:6333"]
59	    env:
60	      # loopback host keeps the data-sovereignty guard satisfied; creds are redacted on error
61	      ASSAY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL: postgresql://[REDACTED]@localhost:5432/assay
62	    steps:
63	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
64	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
65	        with:
66	          python-version: "3.12"
67	      - name: Install (all extras, hash-pinned)
68	        run: |
69	          # Install from the hashed all-extras lockfile so the integration suite executes against
70	          # exactly the audited, pinned versions — closing the window where a compromised patch
71	          # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
72	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
73	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
74	          pip install --no-deps -e .
75	      - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
76	        run: mypy
77	      # Real backends now run: langgraph graph + interrupt/resume, mlflow lifecycle, OTel,
78	      # numpy primitives, the T3 drain/classification (synthetic SDK, no metered call), AND —
79	      # via the service containers — the live Postgres checkpointer + Qdrant vector store.
80	      # Ollama and the metered frontier tier are not in CI and skip honestly.
81	      - name: Integration + unit tests with coverage
82	        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing
83	
84	  # Supply-chain: fail on known-vulnerable dependencies (a security/privacy engine must not ship
85	  # with advisories). The scan runs against the committed, fully-pinned, hashed lockfile
86	  # (requirements.lock) so the audit is reproducible across runs and matches what installs
87	  # (#108, #130, #131, #132, #145, #146). An allowlist file (.pip-audit-ignore, one advisory id
88	  # per line, '#' comments and blank lines allowed) covers accepted/unfixable ones.
89	  audit:
90	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
91	    steps:
92	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
93	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
94	        with:
95	          python-version: "3.12"
96	      - name: Install pip-audit
97	        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip pip-audit
98	      - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
99	        run: |
100	          # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
101	          # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
102	          # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
103	          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
104	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
105	          # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
106	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
107	            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
108	          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
109	          # stay in sync too.
110	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
111	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
112	            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
113	      - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
114	        run: |
115	          set -euo pipefail
116	          # --strict: any dependency that cannot be collected/audited FAILS the gate (no silent
117	          # skips). --require-hashes: every pin must carry a hash. Build --ignore-vuln args as an
118	          # array so allowlist entries can't word-split or glob-expand, and read the file
119	          # newline-agnostically so the last line is honored even without a trailing newline.
120	          IGNORES=()
121	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
122	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
123	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
124	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
125	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
126	          fi
127	          pip-audit --strict --require-hashes --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock "${IGNORES[@]}"
128	      - name: SBOM (CycloneDX) from the lockfile
129	        run: |
130	          set -euo pipefail
131	          # Emit a machine-readable software bill of materials for the pinned dependency set so
132	          # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
133	          # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
134	          # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
135	          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json
136	          test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json
137	          echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"
138	      - name: Upload SBOM artifact
139	        if: always()
140	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
141	        with:
142	          name: sbom-cyclonedx
143	          path: sbom.cyclonedx.json
144	          if-no-files-found: error
145	      - name: License gate (deny strong-copyleft in the pinned dependency set)
146	        run: |
147	          set -euo pipefail
148	          # Fail if any dependency carries a strong-copyleft license incompatible with the
149	          # Apache-2.0 distribution (#147). Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a
150	          # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
151	          # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
152	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
153	          pip install --no-deps -e .
154	          python -m pip install pip-licenses
155	          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
156	          python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
157	      - name: Upload license report
158	        if: always()
159	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
160	        with:
161	          name: license-report
162	          path: licenses.json
163	          if-no-files-found: warn
164	
165	  # Single required status check (#F-014): branch protection should require ONLY `all-checks`.
166	  # It fans in every other job, so adding a new job automatically gates merges without anyone
167	  # remembering to update the branch-protection rule — and `audit`/`integration` can never be
168	  # silently dropped from the merge gate by requiring just `core`.
169	  all-checks:
170	    if: always()
171	    needs: [core, integration, audit]
172	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
173	    steps:
174	      - name: Verify all required jobs succeeded
175	        run: |
176	          results="${{ needs.core.result }} ${{ needs.integration.result }} ${{ needs.audit.result }}"
177	          echo "job results: $results"
178	          for r in $results; do
179	            if [ "$r" != "success" ]; then
180	              echo "::error::a required job did not succeed ($r)"
181	              exit 1
182	            fi
183	          done
184	          echo "all required jobs succeeded"
185

content.text: Now let me look at the lockfile and the regression tests.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && head -30 requirements-core.lock && echo "---TOTAL LINES---" && wc -l requirements-core.lock && echo "---BACKEND CHECK (should be absent)---" && grep -iE 'qdrant|langgraph|mlflow|ollama|psycopg|opentelemetry|numpy|langchain' requirements-core.lock | head -20

content.text: Good, no backends in core lockfile. Let me find the tests.

content.content:
180	    p = tmp_path / "licenses.json"
181	    p.write_text(json.dumps(report), encoding="utf-8")
182	    rc = gate.main(str(p))
183	    assert rc == (1 if denied else 0), f"{license_name!r}: expected denied={denied}"
184	
185	
186	def test_ci_test_lanes_install_hash_pinned_not_live_pypi():
187	    # #F-004: the core AND integration test lanes must install from the hashed lockfiles with
188	    # --require-hashes, not a live `pip install -e .[...]` against PyPI (where a malicious patch
189	    # release could execute under test).
190	    text = _ci_text()
191	    assert "--require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock" in text, (
192	        "core lane not hash-pinned (#F-004)"
193	    )
194	    assert "--require-hashes -r requirements.lock" in text, "integration lane not hash-pinned"
195	    assert (_ROOT / "requirements-core.lock").exists(), "requirements-core.lock missing (#F-004)"
196	    # the old live-resolution install of the engine-with-extras must be gone from the test lanes
197	    assert 'pip install -e ".[dev]"' not in text
198	    assert 'pip install -e ".[dev,reasoning' not in text
199	
200	
201	def test_core_lockfile_is_pinned_and_hashed_and_extra_free():
202	    # #F-004: the core lock backs the no-extras lane — it must be fully pinned + hashed and must
203	    # NOT pull in any backend extra (preserving the ADR-0006 dependency-free-core guard).
204	    core = (_ROOT / "requirements-core.lock").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
205	    assert "--hash=sha256:" in core
206	    for backend in ("arize-phoenix", "langgraph", "psycopg", "qdrant-client", "langchain-ollama"):
207	        assert f"\n{backend}==" not in core, f"core lock leaked a backend extra: {backend}"
208	
209	
210	def test_sbom_generation_fails_loud():
211	    # #F-012: the SBOM step must not mask pip-audit failure with `|| true`, and the upload must
212	    # error (not warn) on a missing file — a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
213	    text = _ci_text()
214	    sbom_section = text[text.index("SBOM (CycloneDX)") :]
215	    assert "cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json || true" not in sbom_section, "SBOM masks failure"
216	    assert "if-no-files-found: error" in text, "SBOM/license upload must error on missing file"
217	
218	
219	def test_license_gate_scans_pinned_lockfile_not_live_resolution():
220	    # #F-013: the license gate must scan the hash-pinned set, not a live `pip install -e .[all]`.
221	    text = _ci_text()
222	    gate_section = text[text.index("License gate") :]
223	    assert 'pip install -e ".[all]"' not in gate_section, "license gate still live-resolves .[all]"
224	    assert "--require-hashes -r requirements.lock" in gate_section
225	
226	
227	def test_ci_has_umbrella_required_check():
228	    # #F-014: a single fan-in job depends on every other job so branch protection requires only it
229	    # — audit/integration can't be silently dropped from the merge gate.
230	    text = _ci_text()
231	    assert "all-checks:" in text
232	    assert "needs: [core, integration, audit]" in text
233	
234	
235	def test_ci_pins_uv_version():
236	    # #F-031: uv must be pinned so a format change between versions cannot cause spurious stale-lock
237	    # diffs that block every PR.
238	    text = _ci_text()
239	    assert "pip install uv==" in text, "uv is not pinned in the lockfile sync check (#F-031)"
240	    assert "pip install --upgrade uv\n" not in text, "unpinned uv upgrade reintroduced"
241	
242

26	_PARSE_LOOP = r"""
27	set -euo pipefail
28	IGNORES=()
29	if [ -f "$1" ]; then
30	  while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
31	    case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
32	    IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
33	  done < "$1"
34	fi
35	printf '%s\n' "${IGNORES[@]:-<none>}"
36	"""
37	
38	# The ORIGINAL buggy loop, for the revert-discrimination assertion in the behavioral test.
39	_PARSE_LOOP_BUGGY = r"""
40	IGNORES=""

content.text:
Both tests discriminate. Let me do a final assessment of the fix's appropriateness.

Analysis of the fix and tests:

**Appropriate:**
- The core lane now installs `pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock` then `pip install --no-deps -e .` — exactly what F-004 requires. Previously it would have live-resolved `pip install -e ".[dev]"` against PyPI, where a malicious patch release could execute under test.
- The integration lane uses `--require-hashes -r requirements.lock` (the existing all-extras lock).
- `--no-deps -e .` is correct: all deps are already satisfied by the hash-pinned lock, so the editable install of the project itself adds no live resolution.
- The new `requirements-core.lock` is correctly scoped to the `dev` extra only (ruff, mypy, pytest, pytest-cov + their transitive deps), preserving the ADR-0006 dependency-free-core guard. No backends leak in.
- The audit job was updated to also regenerate and diff `requirements-core.lock` against `uv pip compile --extra dev`, so a stale core lock fails CI — closing the maintenance gap idiomatically and consistently with the existing `requirements.lock` sync check.
- Idiomatic: header comment documents the generating command; uv is version-pinned (F-031) for both syncs.

**Complete:**
- `test_ci_test_lanes_install_hash_pinned_not_live_pypi` asserts both lanes are hash-pinned, the core lock exists, and the old live `pip install -e ".[dev...]"` forms are gone. Verified it FAILS when the core lane is reverted to a live install.
- `test_core_lockfile_is_pinned_and_hashed_and_extra_free` asserts the lock is hashed and free of every backend extra. Verified it FAILS when a backend (qdrant-client) is injected.
- Both tests pass on the current tree and both discriminate a revert.

One minor note (not a defect): I could not run `uv pip compile` locally to independently confirm the core lock is byte-for-byte in sync (uv isn't in the venv), but the lockfile is well-formed, hash-pinned, and scoped exactly to the `dev` extra, and the audit job enforces sync in CI. This is not a confirmer-blocking concern.

input.reasoning:
Independently verified at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.

APPROPRIATE: .github/workflows/ci.yml core lane now runs `pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock` then `pip install --no-deps -e .` (lines 28-29); integration lane uses `--require-hashes -r requirements.lock` then `--no-deps -e .` (lines 73-74). This correctly closes the F-004 window where a live `pip install -e .[dev...]` resolution could pull a malicious patch release under test. `--no-deps -e .` is sound because all deps come from the hash-pinned lock. The new requirements-core.lock is fully pinned + hashed and scoped exactly to the pyproject `dev` extra (ruff/mypy/pytest/pytest-cov + transitives); grep confirms zero backends (qdrant/langgraph/psycopg/phoenix/ollama) leak in, preserving the ADR-0006 dependency-free-core guard. The audit job was extended (lines 110-112) to regenerate and diff the core lock with version-pinned uv, so a stale core lock fails CI — idiomatic and consistent with the existing requirements.lock sync check.

COMPLETE: Both named tests pass on the current tree (2 passed). Revert-discrimination verified by hand: reverting the core lane to `pip install -e ".[dev]"` makes test_ci_test_lanes_install_hash_pinned_not_live_pypi FAIL ('core lane not hash-pinned'); injecting a qdrant-client entry into the core lock makes test_core_lockfile_is_pinned_and_hashed_and_extra_free FAIL. Both tests genuinely discriminate the fix.

Minor non-blocking note: uv is not installed in the local venv, so I could not byte-for-byte confirm the core lock is in sync with `uv pip compile --extra dev`; however the lock is well-formed, correctly scoped, and the audit job enforces sync in CI. Working tree left clean (all probes reverted).

content.text:
Verdict: CONFIRMED.

F-004 is both appropriate and complete. The core and integration CI test lanes now install hash-pinned from lockfiles (`--require-hashes`) then `--no-deps -e .`, closing the live-PyPI-resolution attack window. The new `requirements-core.lock` is correctly scoped to the `dev` extra only (no backends, preserving ADR-0006), fully pinned and hashed. The audit job was extended to keep the core lock in sync. Both regression tests pass and were independently verified to discriminate a revert (hash-pinning removal and backend-leak injection each make their respective test fail). Working tree left clean.
